claim: N instances of one model aren't a richer model — they re-sample the same context→output function N times; divergence comes ONLY from differences in conditioning (prompt, in-context evidence, assigned role, sampling noise, path-dependent history)
tether: This is the load-bearing mechanism distinguishing genuine ensemble gain from echo; it predicts that without differentiated conditioning, multi-instance = correlated noise around one voice

claim: When does running several same-model instances produce something better than one, vs just an echo of the same voice — and what's the dividing mechanism
tether: The framing question Consonance's whole multi-instance + scribe premise rests on; answer was in progress (conditioning differences) but not yet completed
